title: Northern Lights shot with a Drone
creator: OZZO Photography
notes: BAYESIAN-PROCESS CANDIDATE -- Norway/Iceland subgenre-deepening phase (run only after the Kyrgyzstan and Faroe Islands evidence passes concluded, per this task's stated location order). PRIOR before searching: the pack's existing Norway/Iceland coverage (abovehorizon-lyngen-alps-fjord.json, pshepfpv-iceland-glaciers-fpv.json, acopian-guilfoyle-washed-up-reynisfjara-dronie.json, wxperiences-norway-lofoten-islands.json, dronography-iceland-kirkjufell-aerial.json) is entirely daytime cinematic/FPV content; the pack has exactly one aurora-borealis exemplar anywhere (usa-today-aurora-borealis-seaside-town.json), and it is a Massachusetts, USA scene, not tied to any of this pack's target elite-landscape destinations. Stated prior: 'aurora-over-Iceland or aurora-over-Norway drone content is very likely to exist and be well-documented, since both are globally famous aurora-viewing destinations and drone-shot aurora content is a recognized, press-covered subgenre in its own right (evidenced by the existing usa-today record already in this pack) -- if anything the risk is too MANY candidates rather than too few, so the search should specifically favor the single most independently-corroborated one rather than the first found.' EVIDENCE: WebSearch ('aurora borealis northern lights Iceland OR Norway drone footage youtube cinematic 4K') and a DPReview article (https://www.dpreview.com/news/4302015660/) converged on 'OZZO Photography' as a named, press-identified creator of aurora-over-Iceland drone footage -- a materially stronger evidence type than this session's usual oEmbed-plus-WebSearch pattern, since it started from independent press attribution rather than a raw search hit. A follow-up WebSearch ('"OZZO Photography" aurora Iceland drone youtube') returned SIX independent press/coverage sources for this specific body of work: Digital Photography Review, Nature TTL, DroneBelow ('Incredible Iceland by Aerial Drone Specialist Ozzo'), Universe Today, No Film School, and Stuck In Iceland ('Meet the Master of Iceland Drone Videos') -- this is the strongest independent-corroboration evidence found for any single candidate across both of this session's prompt runs (deterministic and Bayesian combined). The creator's own site (https://ozzo.is/drone, fetched this session) independently confirms the specific piece: 'Exploring the Northern Lights in Iceland with a Drone, flying over epic landscape in Reykjanes Peninsula' -- and a direct WebFetch of the Vimeo page itself (https://vimeo.com/180028553, NOT rate-limited, unlike every YouTube watch-page attempt this session) returned the identical description plus concrete technical detail: shot with a DJI Matrice 600 drone carrying a Sony A7sII camera and Sigma 20mm f/1.4 lens, scored with music by Lights & Motion -- a professional-grade platform/camera/lens/music combination, not a consumer drone's built-in camera, and a real, checkable technical basis for scoring above this pack's unvetted-creator floor. Vimeo's own oEmbed endpoint (https://vimeo.com/api/oembed.json?url=https://vimeo.com/180028553) additionally returned a directly-confirmed duration of 106 seconds -- notably, Vimeo's oEmbed exposes duration where YouTube's does not, a genuinely new and useful tooling finding from this session. The video is also cross-posted to YouTube under 'OZZO ICELAND' (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ypR0y7GVMAQ, oEmbed-confirmed author_url https://www.youtube.com/@OZZOICELAND, title 'Northern lights with Drone') -- same creator, corroborating rather than duplicating this record (this record cites the Vimeo original as source_url specifically because Vimeo's fetch surfaced richer, directly-confirmed technical/duration detail than any YouTube equivalent this session). No explicit Creative Commons marking was found on either platform (the Vimeo page fetch succeeded but displayed no license type), so license_category stays the conservative all-rights-reserved default per this project's standing convention -- this is an absence-of-confirmation default, not a positive all-rights-reserved finding. POSTERIOR after this candidate: confidence that 'the pack's aurora/night-sky subgenre coverage for its actual target elite-landscape destinations is comprehensive' shifted sharply UPWARD -- this single candidate is arguably the best-evidenced record added in this entire session (deterministic + Bayesian), combining multi-outlet independent press coverage, cross-platform corroboration, and creator-disclosed technical specs, none of which most other candidates this session had simultaneously. Calibrated at this pack's top tier (near skypixel11_africa_unseen_ellisvanjason.json's 0.8/0.8 and bbc-earth-aerial.json's 0.78/0.75/0.80) on the strength of that evidence; exposure (0.76) is held below sharpness/motion_smoothness specifically because aurora-at-night is an inherently hard exposure case (per this pack's existing usa-today-aurora-borealis-seaside-town.json precedent, scored 0.69 exposure for the same underlying reason) -- scored higher than that record specifically because OZZO's confirmed use of a Sony A7sII (a full-frame, low-light-optimized sensor, unlike a typical drone's built-in camera) is a real, disclosed technical mitigating factor that record did not have. `composite_score` is NOT a call to the repo's real `compute_composite_score`/`default_weights()` (no shell access this session); computed via the documented equal-weighted-mean formula: (0.78+0.76+0.80)/3 = 0.78 exactly. `composition` stays null.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: Not independently reviewed via playback this session. The confirmed 106-second runtime and the creator-disclosed use of a scored music track (Lights & Motion) both point toward a single deliberately-paced short cinematic piece rather than a rapid-cut compilation, which is a real (if indirect) evidentiary basis stronger than this session's usual title-only inference -- still not a substitute for an actual playback-based cut count, so estimated_cut_count/avg_shot_length_seconds/transition_styles_observed stay null/empty.
